message: chore(beads): close ft-gqbpk — mux-server graceful SIGTERM landed in 0f38972b

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4. If build requires CC/CXX overrides, document in README.","notes":"2026-04-07 CyanIvy: install/docs command corrected; targeted docs smoke test passes via rch; clean-host harness now passes and records WezTerm bridge prerequisites honestly; remaining gap is true clean-host cargo-install-from-git validation on Linux/macOS because rch in this environment is classifying cargo install as non-offloaded. Added workflow .github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml for that next step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.274240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.261176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4. CHANGELOG.md updated with v0.1.0 section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.025577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.022546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.706256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.3","title":"Add CI cargo-install smoke test that runs on every push to main","description":"# Goal
Add a CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs cargo install from the repo and verifies the binary works, catching install regressions early.

# Design
- New job in ci.yml: install-smoke
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64), macos-latest (arm64)
- Steps: checkout → install Rust nightly → cargo install --path crates/frankenterm → ft --version → ft doctor --json
- Timeout: 30 minutes (the build is large)
- Cache: cargo registry + target dir for speed

# Acceptance Criteria
1. CI job passes on both platforms.
2. Job fails if ft binary doesn't produce valid --version output.
3. Job completes in under 20 minutes with caching.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.765078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.763741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","regression","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.917903Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-gqbpk","title":"[LOW] frankenterm-mux-server daemon has no SIGTERM/SIGINT handler; abrupt kill skips blob-lease cleanup","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #7 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
`crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs::run` ends with
`loop { executor.tick()?; }` at line 167 with no signal handler
registered. The process installs no handler for SIGTERM or SIGINT,
so both signals trigger the default "terminate immediately" action
— the loop is interrupted mid-tick and `run()` never returns.

That matters because the cleanup call
`wezterm_blob_leases::clear_storage()` only runs inside the `main`
function (main.rs:66 error path, :70 success path) AFTER `run()`
returns. A SIGTERM that kills the process mid-tick leaves any
in-flight blob-lease storage entries unflushed and, more broadly,
skips any drop-impl cleanup that depends on unwinding.

## Evidence
- `crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs:63-70` — main()
  wraps run() and only invokes clear_storage() on the return
  branches.
- `crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs:167-169` — the
  executor loop has no select-on-signal escape.
- No `signal_hook`, `tokio::signal`, or `nix::sys::signal`
  references anywhere in `crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/`
  (grep confirms zero hits).
- By contrast, `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:15643-15721`
  (`ft watch`) correctly races SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP via
  `runtime_compat::select!` and falls through to a graceful
  shutdown that signals IPC, captures a snapshot checkpoint,
  calls `handle.signal_shutdown()`, and waits for metrics. So the
  fleet has a reference pattern to copy from.

## Impact
Severity: LOW. The mux-server is the WezTerm-vendored subprocess
that backs the mux interop boundary; `ft watch` is the primary
daemon and already shuts down cleanly. The blob-lease leak on
SIGTERM is:
- bounded in size (leases are small and are reaped on next mux
  startup via the `CACHE_DIR` SimpleTempDir lifecycle),
- not attacker-reachable (local signal, already implies process
  compromise),
- invisible to most operators who stop the mux via `ft restart`
  (which takes the `ft watch`-side snapshot BEFORE issuing
  SIGTERM, so the snapshot lane is unaffected).

The visible symptom is a pid-file that lingers after an unclean
shutdown (pid file fd is explicitly `set_cloexec(fd, false)` at
daemonize.rs:140 — intentional for re-exec — but drop of the
lock-holding `std::fs::File` never runs on SIGTERM).

## Fix direction
1. In `frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs`, install a SIGTERM +
   SIGINT handler BEFORE entering the executor loop. Options:
     - `signal_hook::iterator::Signals::new([SIGINT, SIGTERM])`
       polled inside the tick loop,
     - `tokio::signal::unix::signal(SignalKind::terminate())`
       if the executor can be driven from a tokio rt (but the
       current executor is SimpleExecutor; bigger rework).
2. On signal receipt, break the loop cleanly so `run()` returns
   `Ok(())`. `main()` already has the `clear_storage` call on
   the success branch — reusing that path is safe.
3. For in-flight PDU dispatch, the existing
   `dispatch.rs::process_async` already has a graceful-on-EOF
   path; a signal-driven shutdown can simply drop the listener
   and let pending dispatch futures complete to their next
   await point.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- `ft watch` SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP handling: clean. main.rs:
  15643-15721 + 15730+ graceful shutdown with snapshot
  capture.
- fork() close-on-exec: `daemonize.rs:14` is the only direct
  libc::fork site. pid-file fd is intentionally non-CLOEXEC for
  re-exec inheritance (daemonize.rs:140, comment at :137-139
  documents why). Rust `std::fs::File::open` sets O_CLOEXEC by
  default (since 1.10) so `/dev/null`, stdout log, stderr log
  opens are safe. Clean.
- Subprocess zombie handling: `policy.rs:2665 run_dcg` uses
  `child.kill()` + `child.wait()` on every error path;
  daemonize.rs:97 properly reaps the intermediate child via
  `waitpid`. No zombies observed. Clean.

## Severity rationale
LOW (P2): does not reach untrusted input, does not leak secrets,
bounded impact. Worth fixing for operator hygiene ("`ft stop` then
pidfile lingers, confusing the next restart") but not urgent.

## Related
- Upstream WezTerm mux-server pattern; this crate imports the
  shape wholesale. Any fix should be scoped to avoid divergence
  surprise on future WezTerm rebases.
- ft-lokam (e790f481) — same audit pass surfaced the diag cwd
  leak; unrelated to this signal-handling gap.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #7.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:57:51.830641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:57:51.830641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cleanup","mux-server","security","signals"]}

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #7.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:57:51.830641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:05:02.750277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:05:02.747942Z","close_reason":"Closed by 0f38972b. Added async-signal-safe SIGTERM/SIGINT handler in frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs: static AtomicBool SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED + libc::signal registration via install_shutdown_signal_handlers(). Replaced 'loop { executor.tick()?; }' with 'while !shutdown_requested() { executor.tick()?; }' so run() returns Ok(()) cleanly on signal, main() reaches wezterm_blob_leases::clear_storage(), and explicit Mux::shutdown() flushes pending mux activity before cleanup. 7 regression tests cover fresh-state invariant, direct accessor roundtrip, reset helper, SIGTERM + SIGINT handler dispatch, end-to-end poll-loop exit timing (guards against stale-flag-read regression), and idempotent install. No new dep (uses existing libc). Integration test spawning real binary + SIGTERM + exit-code assertion deferred as follow-up (requires config+sockets+TLS harness that does not exist today). sigaction migration deferred until handlers are ever installed post-thread-spawn.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cleanup","mux-server","security","signals"]}
 {"id":"ft-grz8r","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:38.047585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.254960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.253673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
 {"id":"ft-gy9eo","title":"codec::Pdu::stream_decode OOMs on oversized varbincode container lengths","description":"Fuzz target `pdu_stream_decode` found an out-of-memory bug in `codec::Pdu::stream_decode` when varbincode deserialization trusted an attacker-controlled container length and preallocated a huge sequence.

Repro command:
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target-fuzz-codec-dev cargo fuzz run --dev pdu_stream_decode --features codec-fuzz-targets -- artifact /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b`

Crasher artifact: `/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b`
Base64: `DkQEAAQA/////3FxcTBxcf4=`
Hex: `0E 44 04 00 04 00 FF FF FF FF 71 71 71 30 71 71 FE`

Expected behavior: reject the frame before allocation.
Observed behavior before fix: libFuzzer reported `SUMMARY: libFuzzer: out-of-memory`.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:54:02.454303Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:10:43.709103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:10:43.708808Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6d8b9e76: MAX_CONTAINER_BYTES=16MB cap in read_vec + size_hint clamped to 4096 to block visitor-driven with_capacity OOM. Regression test inlines the 17-byte crasher.","external_ref":"/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-h164o","title":"[SEV] mcp_manifest_golden hardcodes 6 shared /tmp/ sqlite paths causing parallel test collisions","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs lines 214, 347, 356, 372, 395, 435 each pass a fixed PathBuf like "/tmp/ft-mcp-manifest-no-db-subset.sqlite3" to capture_manifest(). Tests mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db, mcp_manifest_matches_golden_with_db, mcp_manifest_capture_is_deterministic, mcp_manifest_tool_names_are_unique, mcp_manifest_resource_uris_are_unique, mcp_manifest_tool_input_schemas_are_objects all share these global /tmp paths. Under concurrent agent builds (memory notes 50+ cargo processes, multiple /tmp/ft-<agent>-target dirs running the same test suite in parallel per workspace) two processes hitting the same sqlite3 path race on creation/registration. Even if build_server_with_db does not open the DB today (per the line 346 comment), any change that begins to open/init it turns these into flaky tests. This is exactly the anti-pattern testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks flags: hardcoded global temp paths instead of per-test tempdirs. Fix by using tempfile::TempDir or deriving paths from std::thread::current().id() + test name.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:16.960828Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:27:55.955576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:27:55.955407Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 6 hardcoded /tmp/ft-mcp-manifest-*.sqlite3 paths with isolated_db_path() helper using tempfile::TempDir. Commit 04dc8d76.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
